issue_title: Bootstrap 4
Unable to express header not being present in response
issue_body:
When attempting to translate some python tests to gabbi I (@cdent) was unable to translate a test that was asserting that a particular header was _not_ present in the response.

It's currently impossible to make these kinds of negative assertions (not just of headers, but that's the case that came up) and it's likely we need to make it possible.

Syntax is a confusing issue:

* The current value for `response_headers` is a dict of key (header) value pairs. If we want to say "this header is not present" that doesn't fit in that structure.
* If we want to express this thing (e.g. header) is present but does _not have value X_ that is semantically different from expressing not being present. We need to think about both when designing to avoid suboptimal grammar for both.

issue_title: inconsistent error handling for unknown test keys
issue_body:
```yaml
tests:
- name: ...
  GET: /
  response_html: ...
```

This results in "GabbiFormatError: Invalid test keys used in test", pretty much as expected.

However, when that key's value is a dictionary, a `KeyError` is raised instead:

```yaml
tests:
- name: ...
  GET: /
  response_html:
      foo: hello
      bar: world
```

This seems inconsistent and undesirable.

issue_body:
Substitution doesn't seem to occur for the key of `request_headers`. For example:

Having this test works as expected, where `$ENVIRON['HEADER_VAL']` is replaced by the value of `HEADER_VAL`.

```yaml
- name: Working test
  desc: This is a working test
  GET: /api/some/endpoint
  request_headers:
    Header-Key: $ENVIRON['HEADER_VAL']

Yet, this test doesn't work as `$ENVIRON['HEADER_KEY']` isn't substituted with `HEADER_KEY`.

```

As a side note, this may have some relevance to #170.

issue_title: MIN_INF过小导致的一个问题
issue_body:
```
(prob, state) = max(
                [(V[t - 1][y0] + trans_p[y0].get(y, MIN_FLOAT) + em_p, y0) for y0 in PrevStatus[y]])
```
为了获取到prob和state，将计算出的概率与前一个状态打包成tuple，但是Python中的tuple比较是按元素比较的，因此当一个从未见过的obs[t-1]出现的时候，总会选择PrevStatus为S或M的状态，即obs[t-1]为BEMS的prob都会变成MIN_INF。而分词中S的概率较小，因此此时通常会表现出不分词，一长串都是M的状态。

issue_title: Centroids output format
issue_body:
Based on the results of running kprototypes on the stocks.csv file included in the examples, I have concluded that kprototypes.cluster_centroids_ represents the centroids in the following format:
    [array([cluster 0 centroid coordinates in numerical space],
              [cluster 1 centroid coordinates in numerical space], ...),
     array([cluster 0 centroid coordinates in categorical space],
              [cluster 1 centroid coordinates in categorical space], ...)]
where the i-th cluster centroid coordinates in either numerical or categorical space is of the form
    [x_i,0, x_i,1, ...]
where
    x_i,0 is the coordinate for the first (i.e. left-most) column of (categorical or numerical) data
    x_i,1 is the coordinate for the second (i.e. second left-most) column of (categorical or numerical) data
   ...
and where the j-th cluster centroid coordinate values in categorical space are elements of the set
    {0, 1, 2, ...}
where
    a value of 0 represents the category value whose name is first in alphabetical order
    a value of 1 represents the category value whose name is second in alphabetical order
    ...
i.e. the numerical values represent the mode (i.e. most frequently occurring) categorical value for the cluster, and where the numerical values shown are chosen by putting the category names in alphabetical order and representing the first name by 0, the second name by 1, etc.

Can you please tell me if my conclusions are correct? If there is documentation that describes all this, I apologize for this long-winded question, and I would greatly appreciate a pointer to that documentation.

Thank you very much.

issue_title: OS X backend not working when called from cron
issue_body:
It was [recently brought to my attention](https://github.com/n8henrie/pycookiecheat/issues/8) that `python-keyring` doesn't appear to be able to retrieve a password from the OS X Keychain when called from cron. Any ideas why?

I assumed it has to do with the keychain being locked, but I'm able to access the keychain from cron using the `security` program (which keyring uses as its default OS X backend), as long as my cron entry first unlocks the keychain, e.g. `security unlock-keychain -p MYPASS /path/to/login.keychain && security find-generic-password -a ACCOUNT -s SERVICE -g >> outfile.log`

Also -- totally irrelevant, but -- what prompted the change to GitHub from Bitbucket? I didn't seen any discussion of it with the relevant commit, maybe I missed it.

issue_title: PushButton change_command button does not remove initial command
issue_body:
When creating a PushButton then the command to execute is a required parameter. It should be possible to change this using the change_command() method, but that does not remove the initial command which was provided during object creation.

The reason that this is happening is that the PushButton uses it's own internal method _on_release() which uses the object variable _command, however when change_command() is used instead it calls the tkinter config method to update the button instead. 

This means that the PushButton it will continue to call the command which was created when the instance was created through the _on_release() method even if the command has been changed. Either the self._command() line should be removed from the _on_release method and replaced by running the self.tk.config(command=_command) so that adds it to the tk button during init, or the change_command() method should be updated so that is stores the command as _command instead of using the button config method.

issue_body:
The `--indent-size` option doesn't seem to apply to dictionaries, lists and tuples. Here's some example input:

```
[
        'foo',
        'bar'
]

{
        'foo': 'bar',
        'baz': 42
}

(
        1,
        2,
        3
)

def test():
        pass
```

Running `autopep8 --indent-size 8` on the input above processes the following result:

```
[
    'foo',
    'bar'
]

{
    'foo': 'bar',
    'baz': 42
}

(
    1,
    2,
    3
)


def test():
        pass
```

As you can see, the indentation on the dictionary, the tuple and the list was corrected to 4 spaces despite the `--indent-size` option being set to `8`. I wanted to submit a PR, but I couldn't find the part of the code which takes care of this.

A quick fix can be:
```
p = subprocess.Popen(['tesseract', '-v'], stderr=subprocess.PIPE, stdout=subprocess.PIPE)
stdout_version, version = p.communicate()
if version == '':
    version = stdout_version
```

I found a couple of other issues on other projects describing the same issue:
https://github.com/rene-aguirre/pywinusb/issues/30
https://github.com/jaraco/jaraco.windows/issues/7

issue_title: Feature request: process group id and session id
issue_body: On Linux, I need to be able to tell if any process in a group (or session) is still alive.  Unfortunately `process_iter()`'s `Process`es don't have this information.

issue_title: Process.as_dict() should raise error on invalid name
issue_body: Right now we can pass any string we want. `Process().as_dict(['foo', 'bar', '???'])` will just return an empty dict. That's error prone as it hides user typos. Instead we should raise a `ValueError`.

issue_title: When the number of processes comes to 3000+, psutil will consume more CPU resource.
issue_body:
Hi!

Recently I find a problem when I use psutil to enumerate the processes in my system and print the detail of them. The problem is that the module psutil consumes more CPU resource when the number of processes  comes to 3000+. It is about 40% on one core!

Here is my code.

```python
def getprocstatus():
    '''
    Returns:
        List of the status of the processes
    Exception:
        For Exception Location
    '''
    PROCTARGET = []
    for pid in psutil.pids():
        try:
            # intialization
            procName = ""
            procExe = ""
            procCwd = ""
            proccmd = ""
            procConn = []
            procUser = ""
            procCrttime = 0

            # setting value
            proc = psutil.Process(pid)
            procName = proc.name()
            procExe = proc.exe()
            procCwd = proc.cwd()
            procCmd = proc.cmdline()
            for cmd in procCmd:
                proccmd += cmd
                proccmd += " "
            proccmd = proccmd.strip()
            procConn = getLISTENConn(proc.connections())
            procUser = proc.username()
            procCrttime = int(proc.create_time())
            tupple = (pid,procName,procExe,procCwd,proccmd,procConn,procUser,procCrttime)
            PROCTARGET.append(tupple)
        except psutil.NoSuchProcess:
            mystr = "getprocstatus: NoSuchProcess exception pid=%d" % pid
            logtofile(RUNLOGFILE,mystr,writeflag=False)
        except Exception, e:
            mystr="getprocstatus: exception %s" %(traceback.format_exc().replace('\n',' '))
            logtofile(RUNLOGFILE,mystr)

    return PROCTARGET
```

When python runs to code of  `proc = psutil.Process(pid)`, it slows down and consume lots of CPU resource.

Environment below:
```bash
ps -ef |wc -l
3926
```

Result:
```bash
ps aux| grep procstatus.py |grep -v grep
root     117983 94.3  0.2 208392 20104 pts/2    R+   14:03   0:05 /LAPPTIV/upshield/bin/python mylib/procstatus.py
```

It means that at that moment the CPU consume of the process of procstatus is up to 94.3%!

SO what can I do for this problem?

issue_title: Feature request: ipdb: add new route before removing old route when changing priority
issue_body:
I recognize this is a special case that would add complexity to the code, so feel free to tell me this is not worth supporting.  However, it would be nice if pyroute2 could change route priorities without introducing a brief window of time where the route does not exist.

pyroute2 currently changes the route in-place if RouteKey doesn't change, and deletes then re-adds the route if RouteKey does change:
https://github.com/svinota/pyroute2/blob/master/pyroute2/ipdb/routes.py#L440
Since 'priority' is part of RouteKey, the route is deleted then re-added when 'priority' is changed, which introduces a brief window of time where the route does not exist.

Unfortunately, the kernel does not support changing the priority of a route in-place, so the route actually does have to be deleted and re-added: http://lkml.iu.edu/hypermail/linux/net/0504.3/0017.html
Note that there are other cases where the route is currently deleted and re-added but could instead be changed in-place (for example, 'oif' could be changed in-place, but pyroute2 deletes and re-adds the route because 'oif' is part of RouteKey) ... however, I don't personally have a need for non-disruptive changes in those cases and my only concern at the moment is 'priority' changes.

In most cases, two routes for the same prefix cannot exist at the same time, so the current pyroute2 behavior is usually correct: If the route must be deleted and re-added, then in most cases the old route must be deleted before the new route can be added, and there is no way to avoid having a brief window of time where the route does not exist.

However, two routes for the same prefix can exist at the same time if they have different priorities, so to avoid introducing a window of time where the route doesn't exist, it is technically possible for pyroute2 to add the new route with the new priority before removing the old route with the old priority.

This can currently be accomplished by users of pyroute2 using something like:
>>> r = ipdb.routes['123.0.0.0/24']
>>> r['priority'] = 200
>>> ipdb.routes.add(r).commit()
>>> r.remove().commit()
However, this requires the use of two separate transactions, which prevents changing the priorities of multiple routes in an all-or-none fashion using a single transaction.

Could a special case be added around https://github.com/svinota/pyroute2/blob/master/pyroute2/ipdb/routes.py#L440 to delete the old route after adding the new route if the 'priority' field has changed?

Right now we're running RandomizedSearchCV on the final model with the same data that we're running it on for the entire pipeline.

issue_title: Add camelCase (and GLFW_ prefixes)
issue_body:
Hi Florian,

I just did a [fork](https://github.com/Zuzu-Typ/pyGLFW-in-camelCase) of pyGLFW where I switched all variable names from lowercase_with_underscores to camelCase (or 'mixedCase') and added the 'GLFW_' prefixes to the macros.
Even though this is not the recommended [PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/#function-names) way, in this case the camelCase approach is very helpful when you want to port C++ code to Python.

That got me thinking...

So. long story short: I would like to ask you to add support for camelCase (and the GLFW_ prefixes) alongside lowercase_with_underscores. It would only have benefits - existing code still works, porting C++ code can be done a lot quicker and pyGLFW would fit the naming scheme of the library it's most likely accompanied by -- PyOpenGL (which also uses camelCase).

Naturally, the user would have to make a _from_ import instead.
`from glfw import *`

Please let me know what you think about the idea.

Thanks for your consideration and best regards,
--Zuzu_Typ--

issue_title: regexp cli argument for wordclout_cli.py
issue_body:
I faced some limitations when using wordcloud_cli.py. The default regexp  \w[\w']+ does not match i.e. "c" "c++", "R", "µC", see [1].  I would recommend to register a new optional command line argument and pass it to the WordCloud constructor.

```
parser.add_argument('--regexp', metavar='regexpression', default="\w[\w']+",
help="specify an explicit regular expression, default is \w[\w']+")
wordcloud = wc.WordCloud(..., regexp=regexpression).generate(args.text)
```
[1] http://fiddle.re/60e26n

issue_title: Play livestream starting at latest possible time
issue_body:
Hi all,
I am trying to use a script to play german public television on chromcast. The given stream offers the ability to skip back up to 30 minutes. When I send it to chromecast, it is allways delayed by 30 minutes, as it seems to start playing from the beginning of the "file". I have to wait a few seconds (15 seems like a working amount) until I can issue the seek command to skip ahead in the stream somewhere close to realtime. That is a little inconvenient. In the code of I can see, that the `current_time=xx` parameter should actually take care of that, but I can't get that to work.

So in code, this works but is inconvenient:

	chromecasts = pychromecast.get_chromecasts()

	cast = next(cc for cc in chromecasts if cc.device.friendly_name == "Kitchen")
	cast.wait()

	mc = cast.media_controller
	mc.play_media('http://live-lh.daserste.de/i/daserste_de@91204/master.m3u8', 'video/mp4')
	mc.block_until_active()
	time.sleep(15)
	mc.seek(1799)

while this doesn't work:

	chromecasts = pychromecast.get_chromecasts()

	cast = next(cc for cc in chromecasts if cc.device.friendly_name == "Kitchen")
	cast.wait()

	mc = cast.media_controller
	mc.play_media('http://live-lh.daserste.de/i/daserste_de@91204/master.m3u8', 'video/mp4', current_time=1799)

How would I start the stream "at the end"?

issue_title: MDF4's save() doesn't handle spaces correctly
issue_body:
# Python version 
3.6.0

# Platform information
Windows 10 Enterprise

# _asammdf_ version
3.0.0dev

# Description
All text after a space is stripped from the destination passed to save() in the MDF4 class. 

Specifically, I have a series of MDF files from a customer that following a naming pattern like "MYFILE [1].mf4", "MYFILE [2].mf4". I am reading them (works fine), updating them (works fine), and saving them. The actual save operation completes successfully, but they are all saved as "MYFILE.mf4", and the file gets overwritten each subsequent save.

EDIT: This only appears to happen if the extension is excluded (.e.g. mdf.save("MYFILE [1]")). If dst includes ".mf4" the spaces are handled correctly.

issue_body:
You may handle this in the Python, but at a quick glance, you aren't returning the original `cd` exit value at the end of your overloaded `cd`. This may be deliberate, but it can cause trouble in scripts that rely on `cd`'s exit status.

Compare [how rvm handles it](https://github.com/wayneeseguin/rvm/blob/master/scripts/cd#L31-43) maybe.

issue_title: CLI format parameter should be optional but is required
issue_body:
It appears the docopt docstring for the cli function is incorrect.
Line 327 has format as a required parameter. Lines 385-389 check if arguments['<format>'] is set and pritns rows.dataset if they are not, which would indicate that the format parameter should be optional. Further, the example on line 344 lacks the format argument.

Suggested fix is to make line 327 as follows:
Line 327: records < query > [< format >] [ < params > ...] [--url = < url >]


Current:
Line 327: records < query > < format > [ < params > ...] [--url = < url >]

Line 385-389:
    # Print results in desired format.
    if arguments['<format>']:
        print(rows.export(arguments['<format>']))
    else:
        print(rows.dataset)

issue_title: status() doesn't honor timeout, hangs indefinitely
issue_body:
In `Etcd3Client.status()` there is the line:

    status_response = self.maintenancestub.Status(status_request)

No 'timeout' value is passed in. Therefore, if it can't connect to etcd at this point (maybe the server can't be reached) it will just sit there for a long time. It will probably timeout eventually, but it's too long.

It's easy to honor the timeout value of the etcd client, though, by modifying this line as follows:

    status_response = self.maintenancestub.Status(status_request, self.timeout)

This works like a charm and gives a proper `ConnectionTimeoutError` exception.

issue_title: Stomp connection hangs forever
issue_body:
Hello, @jasonrbriggs 
I encountered a problem with ```StompConnection11``` protocol during connection establishment. Class ```Protocol11``` implements ```connect``` method with arbitrary parameter ```wait```.
If I create a connection with ```wait=True``` and try to connect to a server socket then the connection waits forever. I created server socket manually using python script:
```
import socket
serversocket = socket.socket(
    socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
serversocket.bind((socket.gethostname(), 61619))
serversocket.listen(1)
(sock, addr) = serversocket.accept()
```
It looks like it is stuck in that place in ```transport.py```:
```
            while not self.is_connected() and not self.connection_error:
                self.__connect_wait_condition.wait(wait_time)
```
because it is not connected yet and no connection error exists.

issue_title: don't re-allocate nameplates right away
issue_body: When a nameplate is released and deleted, the server should avoid re-allocating it to someone else for maybe 10 minutes. This would add some weak protection against an MitM attack: if they guess the code correctly, they'll steal the connection to the first side, but they wouldn't be able to make a connection to the second side until the server releases the channel.

issue_title: mimeparse vs python-mimeparse
issue_body:
Howdy,

While doing some packaging work for Fedora, I noticed that both [mimeparse](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/mimeparse) and [python-mimeparse](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/python-mimeparse) are available on PyPI.  After digging through the commit history, I see that python-mimeparse was originally [forked from mimeparse](https://github.com/dbtsai/python-mimeparse/commit/c4681c0f31215227c9ecc5e7c2883671c02d1ade).  Mimeparse only has one commit after the fork happened.

To reduce the confusion, would it be possible for the projects to be merged back together?  Or put another way, what would it take for this module to claim the mimeparse name on PyPI and become the single official source?

issue_title: Don't require trailing slashes
issue_body:
We run our site without trailing slashes, and has nginx redirect all URLs with a trailing slash to a non-trailing slash version. This however breaks social auth, since all the routes configured require a trailing slash. I've worked around it by not having nginx rewrite the social auth URLs, but is it possible to not require the trailing slash? Would be nice to be able to be consistent about this.

If the social auth apps allow both, you could let your web server redirect to the one you want to use, to avoid having content served on both. Or it could be defined in the social auth settings which version you prefer.

issue_body:
I have a minimal web app that works fine:

```Python
from flask import Flask

app = Flask(__name__)

@app.route('/')
def hello_world():
  return 'Hello, World!'

app.run(host='localhost', port='1234')
```

I can enable https successfully (page views fine this way):
```Python
from flask import Flask

app = Flask(__name__)

@app.route('/')
def hello_world():
  return 'Hello, World!'

app.run(host='localhost', port='1234', ssl_context='adhoc')

from flask import Flask
from flask_sslify import SSLify

app = Flask(__name__)

issue_title: Relax restriction on callbacks from modal dialogs
issue_body:
Some people would prefer to use member functions for their modal dialog callbacks, but this is not safe to clone when handling screen resizing.

One option may be to allow programmers to decide whether they want to maintain the modal dialog over resizing (and so stick to this restriction) or cancel the dialog on resize (and so allowing this restriction to be lifted).  The choice could be enforced through a new keyword parameter on the constructor.

Some feedback on this idea would be appreciated.

issue_title: v3 making tests run slowly
issue_body:
Hey @jirikuncar, thanks for all your recent work on Flask-Security.

I've been having a pretty weird time with the recent v3 release. Our tests now run about 10 times more slowly than before - and debugging suggests it's down to our calls to `/login` endpoint. I saw this in the changelog:

    Upgraded passlib, and removed bcrypt version restriction.

Also profiling suggests 98.9% of a test's time is spent in `<built-in method _crypt.crypt>`.

This all leads me to believe a change to underlying hash algo is causing 1 second-ish pauses on calls to `utils.verify_hash`?

Can you confirm any of this please? :D

issue_title: engineio incomaptible with aiohttp-3
issue_body:
Hi! While debugging strange behaviour of Socket.IO with aiohttp server I've come across an issue in engineio.

You are calling `aiohttp.web.WebSocketResponse.send_str()` in `async_aiohttp.py`, but since aiohttp-3 this method is actually a coroutine, so you have to await it:

https://docs.aiohttp.org/en/stable/web_reference.html#aiohttp.web.WebSocketResponse.send_str

Can you please fix this? I can file a simple PR adding `await` and `aiohttp>3` requirement, but I guess you'd want to support both versions.

Right now I had to downgrade `aiohttp` on my server to workaround this.

issue_title: Better support for choices, null=True and blank=True
issue_body:
I have the following model at my app

~~~
BLOG_POST_STATUS = (
    ('U', 'Waiting for triage'),  # This is the status after we receive the blog post draft.
    ('R', 'Waiting to be reviewed'),  # Blog post is assigned to one staff to be reviewed.
    ('G', 'Waiting to be proofread'),  # Blog post is assigned to be proofread by the community officer.
    ('L', 'Waiting to be published'),  # Blog post will be publish by the community officer.
    ('P', 'Published'),  # Blog post is published and have a URL at the website.
    ('D', 'Declined'),  # Blog post submitted by mistake.
    ('O', 'Out of date'),  # Blog post that wait too long to be publish for any reason.
    ('X', 'Remove'),  # When the fellow decided to remove their request.
)

class Blog(models.Model):
    status = models.CharField(
        choices=BLOG_POST_STATUS,
        max_length=1,
        default="U"
    )
    title = models.CharField(
        max_length=MAX_CHAR_LENGTH,
        null=True,
        blank=True
    )
    published_url = models.CharField(
        max_length=MAX_CHAR_LENGTH,
        null=True,
        blank=True
    )
    tweet_url = models.CharField(
        max_length=MAX_CHAR_LENGTH,
        null=True,
        blank=True
    )

    added = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    updated = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)

    def save(self, *args, **kwargs):  # pylint: disable=arguments-differ
        if self.published_url:
            self.status = 'P'
        super(Blog, self).save(*args, **kwargs)
~~~

After I run

issue_title: Small problem with DRF js client
issue_body:
Hi,

First, thanks for your package.
When using the js client from django DRF, it calls the get_serializer_context() method without any request.
So in views.py, SerializerExtensionsAPIViewMixin class, method get_extensions_mixin_context (line 41), request is None and method fails.
```
if params_enabled:
    query_params = self.request.query_params.getlist(field) 
```
Adding a simple condition solves this.
```
if params_enabled and self.request is not None:
    query_params = self.request.query_params.getlist(field) 
```

this adress is wrong normally the communication between cluster (hadoop-master and slaves) should use 10.0.0.0
now I will explain the problem and the architecture.

I am using the same image that I have create in my 2 hosts:
I have 2 container (slave2 and master) in the same host 
I have 1 container (slave1) in different host 

when using docker in multi-hosts I should use overlay network this produce 2 interfaces inside my containers:
- the local container interface with the network 10.0.0.0 
- and another interface is created to communicate with the host machine using 172.18.0.0

hadoop-master: has address 10.0.0.2  and 172.18.0.2
hadoop-slave2: has address 10.0.0.3  and 172.18.0.3
hadoop-slave1: has address 10.0.0.4  and 172.18.0.2 (because not the same host as the master)

the hdoop cluster communicate with each other using host-names created by docker and this uses 10.0.0.0 interface.

So the reservation listening address 172.18.0.2 should instead be 10.0.0.xxx address

I think there is a problem with my spark or yarn configuration but I am not sure. So i was asking what are you using in order to get the interface where to listen for reservation.

Note1: when creating the cluster inside the same host everything is working fine (this is because they can all see the 172.18.0.0 network interface).

Note 2: I think that the node manager is listening  on all interfaces 0.0.0.0 but not sure

issue_title: Document context handler does not allow unspecified _id
issue_body:
It appears that the Document context handler does not behave as described in the documentation.  According to [the docs](http://python-cloudant.readthedocs.io/en/latest/document.html), the document ID is optional to specify (allowing the database to set the _id itself.)  However the library does not seem to work this way.
- Cloudant (python-cloudant) version(s) that are affected by this issue: 2.7.0
- Python version: 2.7.10

```
from cloudant.document import Document
from cloudant.client import Cloudant

db = client['testdb']
with Document(db) as doc:
    doc['field1'] = "foo"
    doc['field2'] = "bar"
```

Produces an exception:

```

issue_title: create_database checks existence twice (3 RTTs)
issue_body:
I was doing some testing and noticed that the `create_database` call makes two `GET` requests before the `PUT` to create a remote database.
This is what happens:

1. `client.create_database()` calls `database.exists()` which does a `GET`
1. If it didn't exist `client.create_database()` calls `database.create()`
1. `database.create()` calls `exists()` which does another `GET`
1. If the  `database.create()` finally does the `PUT`.

I think it could be beneficial to replace these three round trips with a single request to `PUT` given that if the database already exists a `412` is returned.
There is some complication in that currently `database.create()` would not throw for the `412` response case, but `client.create_database` optionally needs to throw.

- Cloudant (python-cloudant) version(s) that are affected by this issue.
    - 2.0.0b2 (probably others too)
- Python version 
    - any (tested on 2.7)
- A small code sample that demonstrates the issue.
```python
client.create_database('abc')
```

issue_title: Upsampling: lack of arguments crashes the kernel
issue_body:
Python 2.7, mxnet v '0.11.0'

Very simply, if sym.UpSampling is not called with the appropriate parameters, MxNet crashes without meaningful error messages.

This will crash:
`mx.sym.UpSampling(data=body, scale=4, sample_type='bilinear', workspace=workspace)`

But this is fine:
`mx.sym.UpSampling(data=body, scale=4, num_filter=3, sample_type='bilinear', workspace=workspace)`

Difference being that I added the num_filter argument 


A full network example

```
data = mx.sym.Variable(name='data')
body = mx.sym.BatchNorm(data=data, fix_gamma=True, eps=2e-5, momentum=bn_mom, name='deco_bn_data')
body = mx.sym.Convolution(data=body, num_filter=64, kernel=(7, 7), stride=(2,2), pad=(3, 3), 
                          no_bias=True, name="deco_conv0", workspace=workspace)
body = mx.sym.BatchNorm(data=body, fix_gamma=False, eps=2e-5, momentum=bn_mom, name='deco_bn0')
body = mx.sym.LeakyReLU(data=body, act_type="leaky", slope=0.02, name='deco_lrelu0')
body = mx.sym.Pooling(data=body, kernel=(3, 3), stride=(2,2), pad=(1,1), pool_type='max', name='deco_pooling')
body = mx.sym.Convolution(data=body, num_filter=3, kernel=(1, 1), stride=(1,1), pad=(0, 0), 
                          no_bias=True, name="deco_toRGB", workspace=workspace)
body = mx.sym.UpSampling(data=body, scale=4, num_filter=3, sample_type='bilinear', workspace=workspace)
test_net = mx.sym.Convolution(data=body, num_filter=1, kernel=(4, 4), stride=(3,3), pad=(0, 0))
test_net = mx.symbol.FullyConnected(data=test_net, num_hidden=10, name='fc1')
test_net = mx.symbol.SoftmaxOutput(data=test_net, name='softmax')
```

issue_title: Performance regression in augmentation
issue_body:
## Description
There'a big performance regression (slowing down from ~5000 samples/sec to ~3000 samples/sec for Resnet50 on Imagenet. This is linked to this PR https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/pull/11027 

What the PR tries to do itself is not problematic, I can get 5k samples/sec with an older commit d37f3a3e63cef5a79c3e673cec30e70f8bf83b3e  on that PR from May24. But in the form it got merged in there's a big slowdown.

## Environment info (Required)

Package used (Python/R/Scala/Julia): Python 3

## Build info (Required if built from source)
pip nightly (mxnet-cu90-1.3.0b20180627) , as well as built from source from master any commit after the above PR got merged

MXNet commit hash: N/A

Build config: Tried with and without USE_LIBJPEG_TURBO, using that increases the speed a bit (~3500), but still much slower than before. Also enabled USE_CUDA, USE_CUDNN

## Error Message:
N/A

## Steps to reproduce
```python train_imagenet.py --gpus 0,1,2,3,4,5,6,7 --batch-size 2048 --dtype float16 --network resnet-v1b --data-nthreads 40 --optimizer sgd --data-train /media/ramdisk/pass-through/train-passthrough.rec --data-train-idx /media/ramdisk/pass-through/train-passthrough.idx --data-val /media/ramdisk/pass-through/val-passthrough.rec --data-val-idx /media/ramdisk/pass-through/val-passthrough.idx ```

I've tried to profile it and see what might be wrong with the tool perf. It looks like opencv is causing a wait for some reason. Please see figure 3

issue_title: Default Weak caching is too strong
issue_body:
The current weak caching defaults result in browsers displaying stale content if only data has changed that is not catalogued. Like every image. I think it is a bug and but I only file a bug for now because maybe there was a reason.
Background:
WeakCaching works with ETags, one of the things etags contain are a sort of serial number of the catalog. But nothing like last modified of the object itself. If I modify an object and change only an image, for example, the catalog finds that no indexable data has been changed and the catalog does not get updated. The ETag stays the same and we are delivering stale content.
https://github.com/plone/plone.app.caching/blob/master/plone/app/caching/profiles/without-caching-proxy/registry.xml#L16

Maybe when plone.app.caching was developing, the catalog was updated even in such a case?
@optilude

issue_title: OpenGEXImporter: Segfault when LightObject is not referenced from LightNode
issue_body:
Hello everybody,

I am integrating assimp for https://github.com/mosra/magnum-plugins/ and found the OpenGEXImporter to crash on our [test file for lights](https://github.com/mosra/magnum-plugins/blob/master/src/MagnumPlugins/OpenGexImporter/Test/light.ogex).

The issue here is that the LightObject structures are not referenced by any LightNode, but the `m_currentLight` member is initialized only on encounter of a LightNode [here](https://github.com/assimp/assimp/blob/master/code/OpenGEXImporter.cpp#L576), whereas handling the LightObject will try to set `m_currentLight->mType` [here](https://github.com/assimp/assimp/blob/master/code/OpenGEXImporter.cpp#L610-L616), even if unreferenced.

I checked the OpenGEX documentation to make sure that our file is valid, and while a LightNode is required to contain a reference to a LightObject structure, a LightObject is not required to be referenced by anything.

Cheers, Jonathan.

issue_title: Integrate with Celery's Configuration
issue_body:
## Current

We support native configuration from Django and Flask (bridging their config locations with Scout's), but not yet for Celery.

You can do:

```
ScoutConfig.set({ 'key': '...', 'name': 'MyApp', '': '', 'monitor': True)

# or as kwargs:
ScoutConfig.set(key='...', name='MyApp', monitor=True)

# or one by one:

ScoutConfig.set(key='...')
ScoutConfig.set(name='MyApp')
ScoutConfig.set(monitor=True)
```

Or you can use environment variables (same names, but capitalized and prefixed with `SCOUT_`: `SCOUT_KEY`, `SCOUT_NAME`, `SCOUT_MONITOR`)


## Desired

We should read from Celery's `app.conf.*` and move any Scout configs over into the agent.

http://docs.celeryproject.org/en/latest/userguide/application.html#configuration

Be sure to support both direct, and from_object forms of config.

issue_title: Open source s2protocol (+mpq parser) implementation in Java and Go
issue_body:
Hello Everyone,

I'd like to announce that I have implemented / ported the `s2protocol` library to **Java** and **Go** languages. They are available as open-source projects on Github.

Java implementation is part of the open source [Scelight](https://github.com/icza/scelight) project (which is much more than just a replay parser, it also gives a high-level api):

**Java MPQ parser:** https://github.com/icza/scelight/tree/master/src-app-libs/hu/belicza/andras/mpq
**Java s2protocol:** https://github.com/icza/scelight/tree/master/src-app/hu/scelight/sc2/rep/s2prot

Go implementation is a standalone project for both MPQ and s2protocol implementation:

**Go MPQ parser:** https://github.com/icza/mpq
**Go s2protocol:** https://github.com/icza/s2prot

The Go implementation is fairly new and will improve over time.

Hope this helps others. Should you have any questions regarding these libraries, I'd be happy to answer them.

Have fun coding and parsing replays. Don't forget to star / watch if you like them.

issue_title: Python3 issues
issue_body:
Not-necessarily-exhaustive list of Python 3 issues:

* `urllib2` module is gone - is replaced by `urllib.request` and `urllib.error`

issue_title: Use flat namespace for hal_data instead of nested dictionaries
issue_body:
I'm sure there was a reason I didn't do this before... but basically, right now we access the data like so:

```
hal_data['pdp']['has_source']
hal_data['solenoid'][0]['value']
... etc
```

I want to change it to this instead:

```
hal_data['pdp.has_source']
hal_data['solenoid.0.value']
```

This will make a few things easier to do, in particular it'll be significantly easier to create diffs of the hal_data if you maintained separate sets of dictionaries for I/O, as it would just be `dict.update()` to copy the updates over. This would be useful if trying to ship the data over a websocket, as you would only need to send changes.

There also would be fewer dictionary lookups in the simulator execution, which is probably a good thing. We could cache a lot of the keys in the fake port objects and such so that we're not constantly computing 

For backwards compatibility, we could define a dictionary shim that would map the existing dictionaries to the new keys (and this might be useful for teams writing custom physics... ).

If you have opinions, state them here. I'm not 100% sold on it yet.

issue_title: Absolute paths for -e . in lock -r
issue_body:
Since 2018.6.25, `pipenv install --dev -e .` gets resolved to absolute paths when running `pipenv lock --requirements --dev >dev-requirements.txt`.

That has the downside, that that .txt is only usable by the person who locked the dependencies if you (like us and I’m sure many more) use Pipenv to manage your deps, but use standard .txt files to deploy/tox.

I’m sure you had good reasons to change this behavior but it would be great to have an option to get the old back.

issue_title: wrong usage of certifi
issue_body:
https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/blob/master/setuptools/ssl_support.py#L245 
This line fetches the install directory of `certifi` and extracts file `cacert.pem` from it.

Although `certifi` labels itself as a trust root source, due to its commonness, it is a good vector for injecting OS-provided trust root for a huge number of Python packages at the same time. At SUSE, we patch the `certifi.where()` method to return the system trust root bundle and remove the installed trust root. That obviously cannot work when a package accesses the pem file directly.

It is also fragile: `certifi` could rename their pem bundle at any time, because it's not part of their API.

The line should be replaced with:
```
import certifi
return certifi.where()
```

I'll try to send a PR when I get to it.

issue_title: Write documentation
More Support for Parsing dates with Timezones
issue_body:
dateparser supports timezones in abbreviated standard formats, *UTC* or *GMT* offsets and in regional formats like *'Australia/Sydney'*. 

```
   >>> dateparser.parse('15 May 1972 10:15 pm',settings={'TIMEZONE':'IST','RETURN_AS_TIMEZONE_AWARE':True})
   datetime.datetime(1972, 5, 15, 22, 15, tzinfo=<StaticTzInfo 'IST'>)
   >>> dateparser.parse('15 May 1972 10:15 pm',settings={'TIMEZONE':'UTC+5:00','RETURN_AS_TIMEZONE_AWARE':True})
   datetime.datetime(1972, 5, 15, 22, 15, tzinfo=<StaticTzInfo 'UTC\+05:00'>)
   >>> dateparser.parse('15 May 1972 10:15 pm',settings={'TIMEZONE':'Africa/Windhoek','RETURN_AS_TIMEZONE_AWARE':True})
   datetime.datetime(1972, 5, 15, 22, 15, tzinfo=<DstTzInfo 'Africa/Windhoek' SAST+2:00:00 STD>)
```
It also supports parsing date string with timezone, but timezone needs to be in standard abbreviated format or as UTC or GMT offset.

   ```
   >>> dateparser.parse('1 nov 1976 12:34 pm EDT',settings={'RETURN_AS_TIMEZONE_AWARE':True})
   datetime.datetime(1976, 11, 1, 12, 34, tzinfo=<StaticTzInfo 'EDT'>)
   >>> dateparser.parse('1 nov 1976 12:34 pm utc+7',settings={'RETURN_AS_TIMEZONE_AWARE':True})
   datetime.datetime(1976, 11, 1, 12, 34, tzinfo=<StaticTzInfo 'UTC\+07:00'>)
   ```

I think dateparser should support timezones in long standard format. It does not support regional or long formats for timezones included in date string. Also there is no support for parsing dates with timezones in other languages except English. The following dates should also be parsed in a way as mentioned below.
   ```
   >>> dateparser.parse('15 May 1972 10:15 pm',settings={'TIMEZONE':'India Standard Time','RETURN_AS_TIMEZONE_AWARE':True})
   datetime.datetime(1972, 5, 15, 22, 15, tzinfo=<StaticTzInfo 'IST'>)
   >>> dateparser.parse('1 nov 1976 12:34 pm Australia/Sydney')
   datetime.datetime(1976, 11, 1, 12, 34, tzinfo=<DstTzInfo 'Australia/Sydney' AEDT+11:00:00 DST>)
   >>> dateparser.parse('19 june 1984 10:06 am Pacific Standard Time')
   datetime.datetime(1984, 6, 19, 10, 6, tzinfo=<StaticTzInfo 'PST'>)
   >>> dateparser.parse('अठारह अगस्त 2000 10:06 पूर्वाह्न जापान मानक समय')   ###(Hindi) 18  August 2000 10:06 am Japan Standard Time
   datetime.datetime(2000, 8, 18, 10, 6, tzinfo=<StaticTzInfo 'JST'>)
   ```

issue_title: Inconsistent ScalarEncoder constructor behavior
issue_body:
Buried in `ScalarEncoder._initEncoder()`, there is the possibility for raising a `ValueError` exception suggesting that one of `n`, `radius`, or `resolution` is required, but it seems `n` is always required.  For example, if you pass `n=0`, and specify either `radius` or `resolution` to something non-zero, it raises an exception: `AttributeError: 'ScalarEncoder' object has no attribute 'rangeInternal'`

This passes, resulting in a successfully created `ScalarEncoder` instance:

```python
encoder = ScalarEncoder(3, None, None, name='scalar', n=14, radius=0, resolution=0, forced=True)
```

These fail, raising unexpected `AttributeError` exceptions:

```python
encoder1 = ScalarEncoder(3, None, None, name='scalar', n=0, radius=None, resolution=0.5, forced=True)
encoder2 = ScalarEncoder(3, None, None, name='scalar', n=0, radius=1.5, resolution=0, forced=True)
```

issue_title: BibUpload: unique identifiers
issue_body:
Originally on 2013-06-04

BibUpload currently matches records also via `CFG_BIBUPLOAD_EXTERNAL_OAIID_TAG`. However no check is performed for the uniqueness of this ID. This opens the possibility for introducing duplicates via BibEdit. BibEdit, in fact, is going to send a `bibupload --replace` containing a `001` tag, thus skipping any further matching based on external identifiers.

What should be added is a step where the identifiers included in the incoming records are checked for uniqueness (as it already happens for `check_record_doi_is_unique()`

issue_title: tests: simplify doctest execution
issue_labels: [{'id': 414883316, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio-access/labels/Size:%20easy', 'name': 'Size: easy', 'color': 'bc3cbc', 'default': False}, {'id': 414883285, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio-access/labels/Status:%20ready%20for%20work', 'name': 'Status: ready for work', 'color': '149414', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
The following cookiecutter change:

   https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98

should be propagated to this Invenio module.

Namely, in `run-tests.sh`, the sphinx for doctests is invoked after pytest run:

```console
$ tail -3 ./\{\{\ cookiecutter.project_shortname\ \}\}/run-tests.sh
sphinx-build -qnNW docs docs/_build/html && python setup.py test && sphinx-build -qnNW -b doctest docs docs/_build/doctest
```

This sometimes led to problems on Travis CI with the second sphinx-build run due
to "disappearing" dependencies after the example application was tested.

A solution that worked for invenio-marc21 (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49#issuecomment-281287103)
and that was integrated in cookiecutter (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98) was to
run doctest execution in pytest, removing the second `sphinx-build` invocation.

This both solved Travis CI build failures and simplified test suite execution.

Note that this change may necessitate to amend the code tests etc so that things
would be executed with the Flask application context (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49/commits/09e98fc18fd5591dc65d806286e8da51b7e384b4).

Trying to install ZEO 5.0 with the PyPy that's on Travis currently fails in a [very confusing way](https://travis-ci.org/zodb/relstorage/jobs/158471721#L261):
```
7.38s$ pip install -U -e ".[test]"
Obtaining file:///home/travis/build/zodb/relstorage
Collecting perfmetrics (from RelStorage==2.0.0b7.dev0)
/home/travis/virtualenv/pypy-2.5.0/site-packages/pip/_vendor/requests/packages/urllib3/util/ssl_.py:318: SNIMissingWarning: An HTTPS request has been made, but the SNI (Subject Name Indication) extension to TLS is not available on this platform. This may cause the server to present an incorrect TLS certificate, which can cause validation failures. You can upgrade to a newer version of Python to solve this. For more information, see https://urllib3.readthedocs.org/en/latest/security.html#snimissingwarning.
  SNIMissingWarning
/home/travis/virtualenv/pypy-2.5.0/site-packages/pip/_vendor/requests/packages/urllib3/util/ssl_.py:122: InsecurePlatformWarning: A true SSLContext object is not available. This prevents urllib3 from configuring SSL appropriately and may cause certain SSL connections to fail. You can upgrade to a newer version of Python to solve this. For more information, see https://urllib3.readthedocs.org/en/latest/security.html#insecureplatformwarning.
  InsecurePlatformWarning
  Downloading perfmetrics-2.0.tar.gz
Collecting ZODB>=4.4.2 (from RelStorage==2.0.0b7.dev0)
  Downloading ZODB-5.0.0.tar.gz (467kB)
    100% |████████████████████████████████| 471kB 2.2MB/s

  Downloading ZEO-5.0.1.tar.gz (328kB)
    100% |████████████████████████████████| 337kB 3.0MB/s 
No files/directories in /tmp/pip-build-KcLvJI/ZEO/pip-egg-info (from PKG-INFO)
```

Locally I tracked it down to this command failing:
```
$ python setup.py egg_info --egg-base pip-egg-info
This version of ZEO requires Python 2.7.9 or higher
```

(PyPy 2.5.0 claims to be Python 2.7.8.)

pip naturally buries this output by default. What's worse, though is that, when the script exits like that, it exits with a successful exit code:
```
$ python setup.py egg_info --egg-base pip-egg-info
This version of ZEO requires Python 2.7.9 or higher
$ echo $?
0
```

(I'm guessing that allowing Python 2.7.8 is not going to happen because SSL. So I'll have to pin versions back just on Travis, just for PyPy.)

However, if we can make the exit code a failure code then pip will at least bail out early with a reasonable error message (I edited the script to sys.exit(1) instead of 0):
```
$ pip install  ZEO-5.0.1.tar.gz
Processing ./ZEO-5.0.1.tar.gz

    This version of ZEO requires Python 2.7.9 or higher

    ----------------------------------------

```

issue_title: using JSONWebTokenAuthentication with DRF IsAuthenticatedOrReadOnly permission throws 401 on GET
issue_body:
If I'm trying to build rest endpoints (single view) that allow both safe (GET) and unsafe methods, whilst relying only on JSONWebTokenAuthentication as authentication class, I get a 401 on GET instead of the request succeeding. 

Reason for this is that JSONWebTokenAuthentication throws an exception if the token is invalid instead of not setting request.user.

https://github.com/GetBlimp/django-rest-framework-jwt/blob/master/rest_framework_jwt/authentication.py#L36

The default behavior for SessionAuthentication in django is to simply not set user on request, leaving the request unauthenticated and letting the permissions do their thing after.

There are several ways around this, as far as I can tell:

1.  Do not send Authenticate header from the client for the GET requests.
2. Build a separate view for the GET endpoints i want to expose and set authentication_class explicitly to not try to authenticate.   

None of these are however ideal for several reasons:

1. What if I want custom behavior on the GET endpoint depending on wether I'm authenticated or not.
2. It's nice and efficient(and RESTFUL) to use a single view with post/get/put/delete for a resource. 
3. It doesn't tie in nicely with DRF's or django's permissions. 

I'm not sure wether this was built on purpose because of the JWT standard but It would be nice to be able to control this behavior from setting perhaps? OR maybe some other elegant solution.

issue_title: Very poor compression ratio on TriMesh binary streams compared to LZMA2
issue_body:
I've long been a promonent of integrating LZMA2/LZMA into the browsers because of its incredible effectiveness for compressing binary data streams.  When I saw Brotli I thought that this was likely going to be just as good.  It isn't actually great.

I am a major contributor to both the http://ThreeJS.org as well as the http://Clara.io online 3D editor.  One of the biggest issues we run into is the size of mesh downloads.  Right now we are using LZMA.js scripts to do the decompression in worker threads, but this isn't optimal, especially on mobile.

For example, this real-world large-ish binary trimesh stream, very typical:

https://d3ijcvgxwtkjmf.cloudfront.net/a4c3c7313b7bdeb68ad46a7e1b761f38z?filename=object-53-batman-tumbler-lw8-12.bingeom

The original size once downloaded is 6,779,000 bytes (be careful, this stream may be delivered as a gzipped http response.)

Here are the compression results:

* LZMA
  * Normal: 921,600 bytes
  * Ultra: 920,147 bytes
* GZip
  * Normal: 2,296,362 bytes
  * Ultra: 2,258,967 bytes
* Brotli
  * Normal and Ultra: 1,513,459 bytes.

Brotli is significantly less effective that LZMA in this case -- not just a little but by a huge margin.

What this means is that we can not replace our LZMA.js scripts with Brotli support.  This is pretty bad for us in the 3D community as we are still stuck with JavaScript-based decompression.

issue_title: viewbox parameter in osm backend is used wrongly
issue_body:
There are two serious issues with your use of the viewbox parameter in the nominatim backend:

1. It is wrongly formatted. There are brackets around the coordinates which cause some random interpretation of the coordinates on the server side.
2. The viewbox parameter is always added. This is bad because the viewbox parameter changes the search query to a geographically restricted query. It changes which results are shown and in which order they appear. The fact that the default spans the entire planet means that you get the results pretty much a random order instead in order of their importance. viewbox should only be added when explicitly requested.

issue_title: Minimum log format expected by sentry/raven-python
issue_body:
Hello,this is probably a documentation error.

What is the minimum log format expected by sentry/raven-python?

In the raven python documentation, there are several formats, and I could not figure out what parameters are required and at what position within the log.

for example, in the [logging documentation](https://docs.sentry.io/clients/python/integrations/logging/), this format is used in the configuration example

```
[%(asctime)s][%(levelname)s] %(name)s %(filename)s:%(funcName)s:%(lineno)d | %(message)s
```

but in [raven-python django logging docs](https://docs.sentry.io/clients/python/integrations/django/#integration-with-logging), use another

```
%(levelname)s %(asctime)s %(module)s %(process)d %(thread)d %(message)s
```

[raven-python pyramid](https://docs.sentry.io/clients/python/integrations/pyramid/)
```
%(asctime)s,%(msecs)03d %(levelname)-5.5s [%(name)s] %(message)s
```

In short, I want to know how I can configure the `formatters` used by python-raven, so that the `formatter` provides as much information as possible for python-raven/Sentry, and in the end, make it possible to use the Sentry reports in their entirety, without a missing informations.

issue_title: AWS API Gateway, Lambda & DynamoDB
issue_body:
Would be interesting to have another server mode that could run this as on AWS Lambda behind API Gateway, loading its YAML files from another datasource such as DynamoDB or S3 (could adding yaml files/datasources be pluggable perhaps rather than filesystem?)

https://zappa.gun.io/ does something similar with Flask, though it creates all the API Gateway configuration - something which Terraform and Apex can also do, but in a lighter weight deployment mechanism (ie without virtualenv) and with more control.

API Gateway of course now has Swagger import also, but this has to be done upfront with creation of API Gateway infrastructure so less dynamic in nature than what Connexion could potentially offer (plus ability to run APIs locally or on non-serverless based VMs/containers as it currently does)

Any thoughts on the feasibility / interest on this appreciated

    from dynet import *

    trainer = AdamTrainer(model)
    for _ in range(2):
        p = model.add_parameters((1,))
        x = parameter(p)

issue_title: Multiple Vulnerabilities in ShadowSocks
issue_body:
X41 D-Sec GmbH Security Advisory: X41-2017-008

Multiple Vulnerabilities in ShadowSocks
=======================================

Overview
--------
Confirmed Affected Versions: Latest commit 2ab8c6b on Sep 6
Confirmed Patched Versions: N/A
Vendor: Shadowsocks
Vendor URL: https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks/tree/master
Vector: Network
Credit: X41 D-Sec GmbH, Niklas Abel
Status: Public
Advisory-URL: https://www.x41-dsec.de/lab/advisories/x41-2017-008-ShadowSocks//


Summary and Impact
------------------
Several issues have been identified, which allow attackers to manipulate log
files, execute commands and to brute force Shadowsocks with enabled
autoban.py brute force detection. Brute force detection from autoban.py does
not work with suggested tail command. The key of captured ShadowSocks
traffic can be brute forced.


Product Description
-------------------
Shadowsocks is a fast tunnel proxy that helps you bypass firewalls.



Log file manipulation
=====================
Severity Rating: Medium
Confirmed Affected Versions: Latest commit 2ab8c6b on Sep 6
Confirmed Patched Versions: N/A
Vector: Network
CVE: not yet issued
CWE: 117
CVSS Score: 4.3
CVSS Vector: CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:L/A:N

Summary and Impact
------------------
Log file manipulation is possible with a manipulated hostname, sent to the
server from a client, even if Shadowsocks is as quiet as possible
with "-qq".

Therefore a string like "\nI could be any log entry\n" could be sent as
hostname to Shadowsocks. The server would log an additional line with 
"I could be any log entry".


Workarounds
-----------
There is no workaround available, do not trust the logfiles until a patch is
released.



Command Execution
=================
Severity Rating: Critical
Confirmed Affected Versions: Latest commit 2ab8c6b on Sep 6
Confirmed Patched Versions: N/A
Vector: Network
CVE: not yet issued
CWE: 78
CVSS Score: 9.0
CVSS Vector: CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:H


Summary and Impact
------------------
When the brute force detection with autoban.py is enabled, remote attackers
are able to execute arbitrary commands.

Command execution is possible because of because of line 53 "os.system(cmd)"
in autoban.py, which executes "cmd = 'iptables -A INPUT -s %s -j DROP' %
ip". The "ip" parameter gets parsed from the log file, whose contents can be
controlled by a third party sending unauthenticated packets.


Proof of Concept
----------------
When, a string like "can not parse header when ||ls&:\n" is sent as host
name to Shadowsocks, it would end up in the logfile and lead to the
execution of "ls".
Autoban.py does not execute commands with spaces due to internal
sanitization. A requested hostname like:
" can not parse header when ||ls&:\ntouch /etc/evil.txt\nexit\ncan not parse
header when ||/bin/bash</var/log/shadowsocks.log&:\n"
could be used to work around this limitation. It writes the command
"touch /etc/evil.txt" into the logfile and executes it with
"/bin/bash</var/log/shadowsocks.log".
The exit; command is an important factor, without it an unbounded recursion
would occur leading to a DoS.


Workarounds
-----------
No workaround available, do not use autoban.py.



Lack of Bruteforce detection through autoban.py
===============================================
Confirmed Affected Versions: Latest commit 2ab8c6b on Sep 6
Confirmed Patched Versions: N/A


Summary and Impact
------------------
The brute force detection autoban.py does not work at all with the suggested
tail command, suggested at
https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks/wiki/Ban-Brute-Force-Crackers.

The command "python autoban.py < /var/log/shadowsocks.log" does work, but
the suggested "nohup tail -F /var/log/shadowsocks.log | python autoban.py >
log 2>log &" does not block IP's.
The "for line in sys.stdin:" from autoban.py parses the input until there is
an end of file (EOF). As "tail -F" will never pipe an EOF into the pyhon
script, the sys.stdin will block the script forever. So the
"tail -F /var/log/shodowsocks | autoban.py" will never block anything
except itself.

Workarounds
-----------
Use python "autoban.py < /var/log/shadowsocks.log" in a cronjob. Do not use
autoban.py until the command execution issue gets fixed.



Bruteforcable Shadowsocks traffic because of MD5
================================================
Confirmed Affected Versions: Latest commit 2ab8c6b on Sep 6
Confirmed Patched Versions: N/A

Summary and Impact
------------------
Shadowsocks uses no brute force prevention for it's key derivation function.

The key for Shadowsocks traffic encryption is static and derived from the
password, using MD5. The password derivation is in encrypt.py in line 56 to
63: "
while len(b''.join(m)) < (key_len + iv_len):
        md5 = hashlib.md5()
        data = password
        if i > 0:
            data = m[i - 1] + password
        md5.update(data)
        m.append(md5.digest())
        i += 1
"

MD5 should not be used to generate keys, since it is a hash function.
A proper key derivation function increases the costs for this operation,
which is a small burden for a user, but a big one for an attacker,
which performs this operation many more times. As passwords usually have
low-entropy, a good password derivation function has to be slow.


Workarounds
-----------
Use a secure password generated by a cryptographically secure random
generator. Wait for a patch that uses a password based key derivation
function like "Argon2" instead of a hash.



About X41 D-Sec GmbH
--------------------
X41 D-Sec is a provider of application security services. We focus on
application code reviews, design review and security testing. X41 D-Sec GmbH
was founded in 2015 by Markus Vervier. We support customers in various
industries such as finance, software development and public institutions.


Timeline
--------
2017-09-28	Issues found
2017-10-05	Vendor contacted
2017-10-09	Vendor contacted, replied to use GitHub for a full disclosure
2017-10-11	Vendor contacted, asked if the vendor is sure to want a full disclosure
2017-10-12	Vendor contacted, replied to create a public issue on GitHub
2017-10-13	Created public issues on GitHub

issue_title: Scaler AWSHelperFn should not override list context
issue_body:
The below test illustrates this issue. The param property is defined as a list of basestrings. This should then allow a list of AWSHelperFn as well (param=[Ref(fake)] below). However, a scaler AWSHelperFn should cause an error (param=Ref(fake) below) but is accepted. Correct behavior would be to raise a TypeError exception.
```
$ cat helper_test.py
import unittest

from troposphere import AWSObject, AWSProperty, Ref

class FakeRef(AWSObject):
    props = {
    }

class Prop(AWSProperty):
    props = {
        'param': ([basestring], True),
    }

class TestHelper(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_helper(self):
	fake = FakeRef('fakeref')
        Prop(param=["fakeref"])
        with self.assertRaises(TypeError):
            Prop(param="fakeref")
        Prop(param=[Ref(fake)])
        with self.assertRaises(TypeError):
            Prop(param=Ref(fake))

if __name__ == '__main__':
    unittest.main()

$ python helper_test.py
F
======================================================================
FAIL: test_helper (__main__.TestHelper)
----------------------------------------------------------------------
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "helper_test.py", line 22, in test_helper
    Prop(param=Ref(fake))
AssertionError: TypeError not raised

----------------------------------------------------------------------
Ran 1 test in 0.001s

FAILED (failures=1)
```

issue_title: Investigate a non-spot strategy for flexible Jenkins worker capacity
issue_body:
Internally at Foursquare, we have a fleet of on-demand workers created but left in the `STOP` state (so they are free minus the EBS volumes attached to them).  A python script run on the Jenkins master node uses a combination of the Jenkins REST API and boto3 to determine if there is queueing, and if so, move nodes from `STOP` to `START` state (and similarly stop nodes when there is no demand).

This is probably a bit more expensive than using spot pricing, but it is significantly more reliable and still quite cost efficient.  Also, the workers come up very fast if they are EBS-only SSDs nodes (instead of our strategy which uses the ephemeral SSD on the machine, which is lost on stop/start and must be reformatted and have repos recloned).

The current issues with the Jenkins spot plugin and market price fluctuation are a deal-breaker for functionality, and unless significantly better heuristics for selecting and bidding on instances based on price and capacity become available, we should probably switch to an on-demand setup.

issue_title: [1.1.0-pre0] jar tool skipping if target includes META-INF/MANIFEST.MF
issue_body:
We have a use case for including an additional `META-INF/MANIFEST.MF` in the bundle.
`BUILD` file:
```
resources(name='tool',
  sources=globs('META-INF/MANIFEST.MF')
)
```
With e1b2d6808f8fe2cdc6964a7f1efd816e2678c70e  switching to `jar-tool`, original `MANIFEST.MF` is skipped, now we get:
```
Manifest-Version: 1.0
Created-By: org.pantsbuild.tools.jar.JarBuilder
```
instead of
```
Manifest-Version: 1.0
Main-Class: com.twitter.scalding.Tool
```

cc @jsirois @mateor

issue_title: git scm server_url origin check is maybe too loose
issue_body:
We're working on setting up an alternate origin for one of our repos at Twitter, and an engineer decided to name the new origin "`temp_origin`":

```
[areitz@XXX]$  git remote -v
origin	https://foo.example.com/ro/some_repo (fetch)
origin	https://foo.example.com/ro/some_repo (push)
temp_origin	https://foo.example.com/ro/some_repo-ci (fetch)
temp_origin	https://foo.example.com/ro/some_repo-ci (push)
```

However, doing so leads to this error when `scm.server_url()`[1] is invoked (where `scm` is an instance of `Git`:

```
Exception message: Unable to find origin remote amongst: origin	https://foo.example.com/ro/some_repo (fetch)
origin	https://foo.example.com/ro/some_repo (push)
temp_origin	https://foo.example.com/ro/some_repo-ci (fetch)
temp_origin	https://foo.example.com/ro/some_repo-ci (push)
```

Our current thinking is that the check for `origin` should be more strict -- maybe something like `/\borigin\b`. I plan on coming up with a patch for this later today.

[1] https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/scm/git.py#L104

issue_title: Shortcut for DidlObject.resources[0].uri
issue_body:
I have noticed that I use `DidlObject.resources[0].uri` quite often and it is also present in the SoCo code multiple times. Hardcoding the index `[0]` in so many places does not seem very good practice to me, I would propose adding a `uri` property to DidlObject which returns `resources[0].uri`:

If, for instance, we were to encounter metadata with two `<res>` tags of which only the second one contains the URI, code at many different places would have to be updated. With an adaptable property, this wouldn't be a problem.

What do you think?

issue_title: Fix JS paths in the Gulpfile
issue_body: It currently is `templateFolder + ` but needs to be `staticFolder + `

issue_title: Git repos on Launchpad are treated as bzr
issue_body: In `charmtools/fetchers.py` the test for a bzr repo matches anything on Launchpad, and the test for Git(Hub) repos only matches GitHub.  This causes git repos on Launchpad to be fetched with bzr, which works, but seems to cause subtle differences, or may not always work.

issue_title: Charm snap doesn't create sub-commands in /snap/bin
issue_body:
It seems that the snap possibly relied previously on classic confinement and set up the subcommands (like `charm-build`) on first run (not sure about this), but there's nothing in `snapcraft.yaml` that creates those subcommands.  So, installing it on a clean system (tested on trusty) results in no, e.g., `charm-build` on the path and attempting to run `charm build` gives:

```
cannot perform readlinkat() on the mount namespace file descriptor of the init process: Permission denied
```

issue_title: charm-version fails when installed via snap
issue_body:
## Checklist

 - [X] Confirmed this is an issue with charm-tools, not charmstore-client
 - [X] Provide versions of tools used
 - [X] Described the feature or ways to replicate the issue

## What version am I running?

I ran the following command: `charm version` and got the following ouput:

```
charm error
charm-tools 2.1.2
```

Output of `snap list charm`:

```
Name   Version  Rev  Developer  Notes
charm  2.2      5    charms     -
```

I am using: Ubuntu Xenial

## Issue/Feature

### I expect/expected the following

`charm-version` should correct report the version of the snap-installed `charm` command as well as the apt-installed version.

### What I got

It instead reports `charm error`.

This is due to [charmtools.version.charm_version](https://github.com/juju/charm-tools/blob/master/charmtools/version.py#L19) only trying apt to find the version.  This could fail, or worse, report an incorrect version.

It should probably detect whether `/snap/bin/charm` or `/usr/bin/charm` is being used (parent process inspection? or just trying one and falling back to the other, I guess) and consult the appropriate system for the version.

Luckily, someone fixed this and built v2.2.3 the same day which is back in good working order.
There will be an investigation ...
Tom

issue_title: Networks option does not support aliases in create service
The api client should send connection upgrade headers
issue_body:
To hint proxies about connection hijacking, docker clients should send connection upgrade headers like the docker cli does.

From https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/api/docker_remote_api_v1.24/#/4-2-hijacking:

> In this version of the API, /attach, uses hijacking to transport stdin, stdout, and stderr on the same socket.
>
> To hint potential proxies about connection hijacking, Docker client sends connection upgrade headers similarly to websocket.

```
Upgrade: tcp
Connection: Upgrade
```

On Docker for Desktop, the proxy that sits between Docker Compose and the daemon will not be aware that the connection will be hijacked. This can lead to an issue where the proxy will install a CloseNotifier and just after that will hijack the connection, which is know to be incompatible. See https://github.com/docker/compose/issues/3685

See also https://github.com/docker/compose/issues/3700

issue_title: Deis database running multiple times resulting in havoc
issue_body:
Hi,

The first thing I noticed was that a new deployment wasn't properly picked up (a bugfix I did still happened). After closes examination (with fleetctl list-units) I saw that v207 and v208 were running concurrently (and apparantly the routers were hitting v207).
I manually cleared this up thinking it was my own fault because I stopped a git push halfway through (although I did that many times before), and then immedtialy did another one.

Then it happened again with v208 and v210 (deis releases at that point didn't even show a v210) and I started looking at the controller

~~~
[2016-02-08 17:13:26 +0000] [115] [INFO] Handling signal: hup
[2016-02-08 17:13:26 +0000] [115] [INFO] Hang up: Master
[2016-02-08 17:13:26 +0000] [583] [INFO] Booting worker with pid: 583

[2016-02-08 17:13:26 +0000] [589] [INFO] Booting worker with pid: 589
[2016-02-08 17:13:26 +0000] [592] [INFO] Booting worker with pid: 592
[2016-02-08 17:13:26 +0000] [590] [INFO] Booting worker with pid: 590
[2016-02-08 17:13:26 +0000] [593] [INFO] Booting worker with pid: 593
[2016-02-08 17:13:26 +0000] [594] [INFO] Booting worker with pid: 594
[2016-02-08 17:13:26 +0000] [595] [INFO] Booting worker with pid: 595
[2016-02-08 17:13:26 +0000] [596] [INFO] Booting worker with pid: 596
[2016-02-08 17:13:26 +0000] [591] [INFO] Booting worker with pid: 591
[2016-02-08 17:13:26 +0000] [563] [INFO] Worker exiting (pid: 563)
[2016-02-08 17:13:26 +0000] [564] [INFO] Worker exiting (pid: 564)
[2016-02-08 17:13:26 +0000] [599] [INFO] Booting worker with pid: 599
[2016-02-08 17:13:26 +0000] [597] [INFO] Booting worker with pid: 597
[2016-02-08 17:13:26 +0000] [598] [INFO] Booting worker with pid: 598
[2016-02-08 17:13:26 +0000] [565] [INFO] Worker exiting (pid: 565)
~~~

was printed many times over.

So suddenly especially because deis releases wasn't consistent I realized I must have had 2 databases running and they would constantly fight over the controllers attention which then would give very unreliable pushes.

I stopped the oldest database (with docker stop), and suddenly my problems went away.

I believe this happened a few days ago when one of my nodes (the one with the oldest database) had connectivity problems for a few minutes. Apparanttly long enough to reschedule the database to another node, but not long enough to kill the database on this node once it came back online..

This is mostly written as referecence to other people using deis v1.12.2 which might have the same problem, as I understand this might not be easy to fix for the LTS release (although it is a critical problem).

issue_title: Deis v2 Design Document
issue_body:
Here is the link to the current design document for Deis v2:

https://docs.google.com/a/engineyard.com/document/d/1twPhGuLwRXMEtLoyQfx8_PEHt1YAEJKmblXqjTK2VlU/pub

It is backed by a Google Doc, but I don't seem to have the privileges to make comments visible. Feel free to leave comments inline here.

This is a work in progress. While the main concepts are solidifying pretty quickly, the details are all still in flux. We're still trying to iron out the details of how we can keep lots of people involved in the design process. If you have some ideas, feel free to share those here, too.

Here's how we went about writing this document:

- In late July and early August, I put together an initial version. It was more of a rough proposal for how we might switch from Fleet to Kubernetes, and reduce some tech debt along the way
- The core maintainers took a look at it and provided a lot of feedback. @bacongobbler and I made some changes. @aledbf and @carmstrong  made several very helpful comments when I was at this point.
- Over the last two months, @bacongobbler @kmala @smothiki and I did some explorations and I drafted out ideas as we had them. @sgoings and @krancour provided more feedback.
- Last month we introduced the ideas at the public release meeting. The document was taking shape at that point.
- A few days ago, @gabrtv @mboersma and I sat at a whiteboard and hashed out the basic architecture (and the splitting Deis into components). I redrafted accordingly, and prepared the document for release.
- At today's public release, we announced this document, and are looking forward to hearing some feedback.

I'll do my best to keep the document at the link above up to date. If I can figure out how to turn on comments, I will.

Set sharding protocol constants and configuration

### How can it be fixed

1. Protocol constants are listed here: https://github.com/ethereum/sharding/blob/develop/docs/doc.md#constants
  Find an appropriate place to set these parameters.

2. Node configuration
    * Related issue: Related to https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/issues/198
    * Sharding only configuration: (Ref: https://github.com/ethereum/sharding/blob/develop/docs/doc.md#client-logic)
        * `validator_address`: "0x..." OR null
        * `watching`: [list of shard IDs]
    * DB setting (TBD): one DB for each shard

We can start `1. Protocol constants` now and leave `2. Node configuration` after #198 is solved or after we start `ShardManager` implementation.

issue_title: Parity (sometimes) returns transaction receipts before they are mined
issue_body:
* Version: 4.4.0

Tested on:
* Python: 3.6
* OS: linux

### What was wrong?

Confirmed once for a locally broadcasted transaction: parity will return the "receipt" of a pending transaction:

```py
In [95]: w3.eth.getTransactionReceipt('...')
Out[95]: 
AttributeDict({'blockHash': None,
 'blockNumber': None,
...
)
```

This goes against the [json-rpc spec](https://github.com/ethereum/wiki/wiki/JSON-RPC#eth_gettransactionreceipt):

>  the receipt is not available for pending transactions

### How can it be fixed?

Option 1:

- Add a new parity_compliance middleware
- In middleware, detect if `blockHash` or `blockNumber` are `None`, and if so, return `None` instead of the receipt

Option 2:

- Check for missing hash or number in `web3.eth.Eth.getTransactionReceipt` and return `None` instead

Upside of 2: easier to add a parity module version of getTransactionReceipt that keeps parity's non-standard behavior.
Downside of 2: any other attempts to use `eth_getTransactionReceipt` directly in internal code might forget to check if block hash or number is None.

issue_title: conda build purge-all not documented
issue_body:
Hi,

My specific situation was this. I am building and installing conda packages locally. I decided to update the meta.yaml to include new dependencies. I then ran the following:
>$ conda build purge
>$ conda build --build-only
>$ conda install --use-local my-package

However it did not pick up the new dependency. From looking at the source code, it seems that purge only cleans the build, and because the build string would have stayed the same as before (I was still testing so I hadn't rev'ed the build or version) I guess it was picking up the old tarball, which has the meta.yaml including deps??

Anyway, I noticed in the source there was a 'purge-all' which removed the tarballs as well. This solved my problem. I can't find any docs or reference to this command, not even in the argparse output.

issue_title: channel_alias inconsistent docs; requires terminating `/` for windows
issue_body:
When pointing conda to an internal repository, the `.condarc` contains `channel_alias` key. This is inconsistently documented:
[channel_alias: https://your.repo/](http://conda.pydata.org/docs/config.html#set-a-channel-alias-channel-alias)
[channel_alias: https://your.repo/conda](http://conda.pydata.org/docs/install/central.html#channel-alias-channel-alias)

For this to work consistently with windows, it should be: `channel_alias: https://your.repo/conda/`
Below is the error we get because of the missing trailing `/`

```
>cat .condarc
channels:
  - anaconda
  - r-channel

channel_alias: https://ec2-xxx-xxx-xxx-xxx.compute-1.amazonaws.com:8443/conda
ssl_verify: False

>conda update anaconda-navigator
Fetching package metadata .....CondaHTTPError: HTTP Error: Could not find URL: https://xxx-xxx-xxx-xxx.compute-1.amazonaws.com:8443/conda\anaconda/win-64/
```

conda likely does an os.path.join so it is platform specific, it ends up looking for incorrect URL:
`https://xxx-xxx-xxx-xxx.compute-1.amazonaws.com:8443/conda\anaconda/win-64/`

issue_title: CONDA_CHANNELS environment variable doesn't work
conda pkgs subdir doesn't track different channels for same package
issue_body:
I created a conda package with the same name in two different channels. I then created two different conda environments. The different environments had this package but it is from the two different channels.
Aftewards, both environments seem to have the correct version of the package, but the conda/pkgs subdirectory now only includes the latter install. I guess this is Ok, but worries me a bit. For managing lots of packages and disk space, I think it would be good to be able to find packages from different channels in the conda install. That is, what I think happens is

1. install mypkg from channelA into envA
creates miniconda2/pkgs/mypkg  say inode is 100 for some header file, mypkg/include/mypkg.h
creates miniconda2/envs/envA/include/mypkg.h with inode 100, using hard link

2. install mypkg from channelB into envB
overwrites miniconda2/pkgs/mypkg  say inode is now 200 for that header file, mypkg/include/mypkg.h
creates miniconda2/envs/envB/include/mypkg.h with inode 200

 I can see that the inode for envA/include/mypkg.h is still 100, so it is still there, (I am on rhel7 linux).

Anyways, maybe this is fine? The application I have in mind is using different channels to keep track of builds on different OS's. We have C++ code I'd like to get into conda packages - I have some understanding of how conda builds things for all the variants of linux (namely build on the lowest common demoninator, I think CensOS 5?) - but I'm hesitant to give up building with a more recent gcc compiler on rhel7. I was thinking of having one channel where we build with rhel5, presumably we can make that available to people offsite, and then another channel where we build on rhel7.
